theme_notes: Touchstones: 2026 FIFA World Cup opening day (48-team debut, ceremony at Azteca in Mexico City); SCOTUS reverses 91-year-old precedent letting Trump fire independent agency heads; Iran nuclear framework fracturing, Tehran threatens to halt talks; Medicare covers GLP-1 weight-loss drugs for first time at $50/month; heat dome bakes US through July 4.
settled: VAR checked it. Zero owed. Go watch the World Cup.
You're square. Even the Supreme Court would struggle to invent a grievance here.
Balance as clean as the heat dome is not.
youOwe: Less than a World Cup stadium beer. That's a special kind of embarrassing.
SCOTUS reversed 91 years of precedent this week. Your excuses aren't that old. Stop.
The heat dome broke records. You broke nothing except the agreement.
Group Stage money. You're still warming up in the tunnel.
Iran's nuclear deal is one bad week from collapse. So are you, apparently.
Medicare covers Ozempic now. Nobody is covering for you.
Two months of GLP-1 copays. You've been shrinking obligations, not waistlines.
Forty-eight nations made the World Cup. Your payment plan didn't qualify.
The heat dome is expected to break by July 4. Your debt has no such schedule.
That's a round-trip to a World Cup host city. They're going. You're pending.
SCOTUS reversed 91 years of independent oversight this week. Nobody is watching your spending.
Tehran threatened to walk out of nuclear talks. Your partner took notes.
The World Cup went to 48 teams this year. Your debt's in the knockout stage with no bye.
Three countries co-hosting one tournament sorted their logistics. Look at the board.
Iran violated the framework. You violated something more personal.
48 nations at the World Cup. 48 different languages for 'you owe me money.'
Tehran halted nuclear negotiations. Your partner is reading the room.
The Azteca opening ceremony had fireworks. This debt has the same energy. Worse crowd.
theyOwe: Less than a match-day program. File the formal complaint anyway.
Three Supreme Court rulings broke records this week. This debt barely registers.
The heat dome is more impactful than this. Still: collect.
One month of government-subsidized Ozempic. They're shrinking in all the wrong ways.
Group Stage ticket money. They watched from your couch. For free.
Iran violated the framework. Your partner is also 'in violation.'
The World Cup expanded to 48 teams and none are as disappointing as this.
SCOTUS overturned a 91-year-old precedent in a week. Your patience has no such precedent.
Two months of Medicare weight-loss drugs. One completely unsubsidized financial anchor.
Round-of-32 seats, easy. They've never made the round of handing money back.
Tehran threatened to halt talks. Your partner said 'I forgot my wallet.'
The heat dome is oppressive, inescapable, and has better manners than this.
Forty-eight nations showed up for the World Cup. Your partner's reimbursement is MIA.
Trump fired the independent watchdogs this week. You've been too polite.
Medicare covers the appetite suppressant. Nothing covers the appetite for this money.
Opening ceremony was at the Azteca. Your goodwill left around the same time.
48 countries. 3 host nations. 1 person who owes you five figures and is on your couch.
Iran, SCOTUS, and the heat dome had a destructive week. Your partner had a more personal one.
